articles.description:
Taco Bell's Slow-Roasted Chicken Nachos Grande features a double portion of slow-roasted chicken (compared to the Cantina Chicken Soft Taco), refried beans, warm nacho cheese sauce, a three-cheese blend (mozzarella, pepper jack, and cheddar), pico de gallo, reduced-fat sour cream, and guacamole, served over a bed of tortilla chips.

It came in a box and cost me $6.99.
The box was filled pretty well with a decent amount of chicken, toppings, and chips. Topping coverage was pretty good, although I always find myself wishing for just a little more nacho cheese sauce.



As with a lot of things at Taco Bell, the combination of flavors was very familiar, as the Grande Nachos aren't much different from Taco Bell's previous nachos in a box (or regular menu Nachos BellGrande, for that matter). Compared to Nachos BellGrande, you get more food for a similar price ($5.99 for the seasoned beef version of both at my local Taco Bell). I would guess it to be a third more food. It's definitely shareable.
What stood out from the Grande Nachos over  Nachos BellGrande was the guacamole and pico de gallo, which added an acidity that the latter lacks (it has sour cream and tomatoes, but they don't add much).
The chicken was also interesting, as Taco Bell's limited-time nachos usually come with beef (either seasoned ground beef or steak). The slow-roasted chicken was tender and meaty with a nice seasoning to it. I much prefer it to the chain's previous chicken, which was milder in flavor and less satisfying texture-wise.



Overall, Taco Bell's Slow-Roasted Chicken Grande Nachos get a thumbs up from me. While familiar, there was a nice variety of flavors and textures to go with a good amount of food for the money. 
Nutritional Info - Taco Bell - Slow-Roasted Chicken Grande Nachos
Calories - 1100
Fat - 59g (Saturated Fat - 9g)
Sodium - 1720mg
Carbs - 111g (Sugar - 6g)
Protein - 32g
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Review: Taco Bell - Slow-Roasted Chicken Grande Nachos

articles.description:
The Sourdough Scrambler makes a limited-time return to Jack in the Box as part of the chain's all-day breakfast menu.

The breakfast sandwich features scrambled eggs, two slices of melted American cheese, and a choice of hickory-smoked bacon strips or a grilled sausage patty on toasted sourdough rounds (the same as you'd get with the Sourdough Jack). You can find my review of the sausage version here.
In addition to a la carte, the Sourdough Scrambler is available in a combo with hot coffee and a hash brown.
The price on Jack in the Box's Sourdough Scrambler starts at $2.99 (it costs $3.49 near me in Los Angeles). The combo meal costs $5.99 near me.
Photo via Jack in the Box.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Jack in the Box Brings Back the Sourdough Scrambler

articles.description:
The Trick or Treat Blast is back at Sonic for the 2025 Halloween season and is available now through the Sonic mobile app ahead of a national launch on September 29, 2025.

The Trick or Treat Blast features a choice of vanilla or chocolate soft serve mixed with Heath candy bar pieces, M&M's Minis, and Oreo cookie pieces, topped off with more of the same candies and cookies.
Prices vary by location, with an average price of $6.39 for a medium.
Photo via Sonic.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Trick or Treat Blast Returns to Sonic

articles.description:
Carl's Jr. is testing the new Really Big Dill Burger for a limited time at participating locations in Central California.

Carl's Jr.'s Really Big Dill Burger (not to be confused with Sonic's Big Dill Cheeseburger) features a charbroiled beef patty, dill aioli sauce, dill pickle fries, pickle crisps, melted Swiss cheese, lettuce, and tomato served on a toasted sesame seed bun. It is also available with two or three beef patties.
The Dill pickle fries can also be ordered as a side.
Pickles are a trending ingredient/flavor right now, and several fast-food chains have embraced the trend, including Sonic (with a pickle menu), Popeyes (also with a pickle menu), Del Taco (with a fried pickle taco), and more.
Photo via Carl's Jr.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Carl's Jr. Testing New Really Big Dill Burger

articles.description:
Jersey Mike's is in the process of removing portabella mushrooms from its menu. Some locations have already used up the last of their stock, and the mushrooms will no longer be available chain-wide by early October 2025.

The removal of portabella mushrooms affects sandwiches #64 to #66, i.e., the Grilled Portabella Mushroom & Swiss (pictured), the Portabella Cheese Steak, and the Portabella Chicken Cheese Steak.
If you're a vegetarian, that leaves The Veggie (#14) as the chain's remaining vegetarian sub.
Portabella mushrooms were first offered at Jersey Mike's in March 2021.
Photo via Jersey Mike's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Jersey Mike's Getting Rid of Portabella Mushrooms

articles.description:
In celebration of its first Curdtoberfest (like Oktoberfest but honoring cheese curds), Culver's reveals new Jalapeno Cheese Curds and the return of the CurderBurger.

Jalapeno Cheese Curds (pictured) will arrive on October 15, 2025 (apparently, the 10th anniversary of National Cheese Curd Day) and will be available for a limited time through October 31, 2025, while supplies last. They're a spicy take on Culver's classic Wisconsin Cheese Curds made with bits of jalapeno pepper and cheddar cheese.
The CurderBurger features a Deluxe ButterBurger topped with a breaded and fried patty of cheese (a blend of yellow and white cheddar). It arrives on September 29, 2025, and will be available through October 14, 2025, while supplies last. It first started as an April Fools' joke in 2021, but then they made it into an actual burger, and this is the fifth time they've brought it back.
In addition to the limited-time menu items, Culver's first-ever Curdtoberfest will include themed decor at its restaurants and an online sweepstakes with "free Wisconsin Cheese Curds for a year" and other prizes.
Photo via Culver's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Culver's Reveals New Jalapeno Cheese Curds and Return of the CurderBurger

articles.description:
Whataburger serves up the new Bacon Wrangler Double as its latest limited-time burger.

The Bacon Wrangler Double features two beef patties, melted Monterey Jack and American cheese, crispy onion strings, bacon, pickles, and chipotle aioli on a toasted bun.
The Texas-based fast-food chain muses that "it just might be the most Texas thing on a toasted bun."
Prices vary, but a location in Dallas charges $9.99 for the burger.
Photo via Whataburger.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Whataburger Serves Up New Bacon Wrangler Double

articles.description:
Moe's Southwest Grill debuts the new Moe Value Meal, a discounted combo meal that includes a junior burrito, a side of queso, and a fountain drink for $9.95. As with Moe's other entrees, you also get free chips, salsa, and dipping sauces.

To promote the new offering, Moe's is holding a sweepstakes game, the Moe Value Meal Game Show, with a grand prize of a year of Moe Value Meals, 1,000 prizes of a free Moe Value Meal, and a reward code for 20% off a Moe Value Meal for all participants. You must be a Moe Rewards member to redeem any of the prizes. The game can be found online at www.moevaluemeal.com and runs through October 5, 2025.
Photo via Moe's Southwest Grill.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Moe's Southwest Grill Puts Together New $9.95 Value Meal

articles.description:
In an odd bit of a switch, Carl's Jr. now serves almost all of its charbroiled and hand-breaded chicken sandwiches on toasted sourdough bread instead of a bun. The only exception is the value-priced Spicy Chicken Sandwich.

Items now served on sourdough include:
- Hand-Breaded Chicken Sandwich (pictured)
- Hand-Breaded Bacon Swiss Chicken Sandwich
- Charbroiled Chicken Club Sandwich
- Charbroiled Santa Fe Chicken Sandwich
- Charbroiled BBQ Chicken Sandwich
Given that fast food sourdough tends not to be sour, the switch amounts to serving its chicken sandwiches on toast.
Photo via Carl's Jr.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Carl's Jr. Now Serves Its Chicken Sandwiches on Sourdough Bread

articles.description:
Crumbl has revealed its limited-time menu for this week, and it includes Hazelnut Sea Salt Cookies and more. The cookie bakery chain celebrates its birthday this week by offering a deal where you can get a 6-pack of cookies for the price of a 4-pack on Wednesday, September 24, 2025.

Here's what you can expect to find on the Crumbl menu from September 22 through September 27, 2025:
- Hazelnut Sea Salt Cookie - A warm browned butter cookie stuffed with hazelnut spread and topped with a drizzle of more hazelnut spread and a sprinkle of sea salt.
- Confetti Cake - A two-tier confetti cake layered with vanilla buttercream and topped with a sprinkle of rainbow sprinkles.
- Birthday Cake Cookie featuring Oreo - A warm cookie made with Oreo cookie pieces, topped with cake batter cream cheese frosting, more Oreo cookie pieces, and rainbow sprinkles.
- Original Pink Sugar Cookie - A chilled almond sugar cookie topped with pink almond frosting.
- Milk Chocolate Chip Cookie - A warm cookie packed with milk chocolate chips.
Additionally, each location has two "secret menu" cookies this week. You can check the Crumbl website or mobile app to see what's being offered near you.
Photo via Crumbl.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Crumbl Bakes Hazelnut Sea Salt Cookies and More Through September 27, 2025

articles.description:
The McWrap has been reintroduced in the US at a single McDonald's location as part of the "Global Favorites" menu at the "global-inspired" restaurant inside McDonald's headquarters in Chicago.

The Global Favorites menu is a rotating menu that pulls McDonald's items from around the world. The current offerings include:
- McWrap Classic (Poland) - McCrispy Strips, shredded lettuce, American cheese, mayonnaise, Roma tomatoes, arugula, and Tomato Onion Sauce, wrapped up in a flour tortilla. It's priced at $6.99.
- McWrap Sweet Chili (France) - McCrispy Strips, shredded lettuce, American cheese, mayonnaise, Roma tomatoes, and Sweet Chili Sauce, wrapped up in a flour tortilla. It is also priced at $6.99. It's a bit different from the Sweet Chili McWrap offered here in the US over a decade ago.
- Big Arch Burger (Global but not in the US... yet) - Two quarter-pound beef patties, three slices of processed white cheddar cheese, crispy-fried onions, slivered onions, pickles, shredded lettuce, and Big Arch sauce on a toasted sesame and poppy seed bun. It's priced at $7.99.
- Biscoff McPops (Spain) - Mini donuts filled with Biscoff Cookie Butter and dusted with powdered sugar. It costs $3.99 for three. McPops also appeared on the menu of McDonald's CosMc's concept.
- Chocolate Hazelnut McPops (Spain) - Mini donuts with a chocolate hazelnut filling, dusted with powdered sugar. In Spain, they're just called Nutella McPops. They're priced at three for $3.99.

articles.description:
Pizza Hut's Chicken Bacon Ranch Crafted Flatzz is a "hand-crafted" personal pizza topped with crispy-fried boneless chicken pieces, applewood-smoked bacon, Ultimate Ranch sauce, mozzarella, and a parmesan oregano seasoning.

I got one for $5 during lunch time (the price goes up to $6.99 after 5PM). I also reviewed the Nashville Hot Chicken one earlier.



The Crafted Flatzz is about twice the size of a Pizza Hut personal pan pizza, but a good deal thinner (and less greasy) with a hand-stretched oval crust. It was somewhat football-shaped, which is fitting for the season, I suppose. It was very crispy on the surface while maintaining a soft crumb inside.
The pizza was cut into eight two-bite (or one big bite) slices with one small popcorn chicken-esque crispy chicken piece per slice.



The mozzarella was nicely melted, but the underlying Ultimate Ranch just tasted like your run-of-the-mill ranch to me. Between the two, there was a moderate creaminess and a herby tang throughout the pizza. The parmesan oregano didn't make much of an impact.
The bacon was a little sparse here, and I could have used a little more to cut against the strong ranch flavor. I feel like it's one of those "your mileage may vary" things, though.

Overall, Pizza Hut's Chicken Bacon Ranch Crafted Flatzz is nice for the price and crust, but leans too heavily on ranch as its primary flavor driver. Typically, when something is called "chicken bacon ranch," it's mostly chicken, then bacon, then ranch. This was more like "ranch chicken bacon." I would definitely choose the Nashville Hot Chicken Crafted Flatzz over it.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Review: Pizza Hut - Chicken Bacon Ranch Crafted Flatzz
